column1: Citation: Graham, J.L., Dubrovsky, N.M., Loftin, K.A., Rosen, B.H., and Stelzer, E.A., 2022, Cyanotoxin, chlorophyll-a, and cyanobacterial toxin genetic data for samples collected at twelve large river sites throughout the United States, June through October 2019: U.S. Geological Survey data release, https://doi.org/10.5066/P99I27T4.

This file contains the cyanotoxin, chlorophyll, and cyanobacterial toxin genetic data for samples collected at twelve large river sites throughout the United States, from June through October 2019. Definitions of column headings presented in the "Cyanotoxins_Chl_Genetics_Data" and "qPCR_Standard_Curve_Information" tabs are presented below.

USGS Station Name, USGS station name where samples were collected (Connecticut  River at Thompsonville, CT; Hudson River near Poughkeepsie, NY; Delaware River at Trenton, NJ; Susquehanna River at Conowingo, MD; Chattahoochee River near Whitesburg, GA; Ohio River at Cannelton Dam at Cannelton, OH; Mississippi River at Hastings, MN; Kansas River at De Soto, KS; Missouri River at Hermann, MO; Trinity River below Dallas, TX; Sacramento River at Freeport, CA; and Willamette River at Portland, OR).
Sample Date, Date samples were collected in M/D/YYYY.
Sample Time, Time samples were collected in H:MM; "NA" indicates lab replicate and no time offset.
Sample Type, Type of sample collected (Regular, Concurrent Replicate, or Laboratory Replicate).

Data qualifiers for Total microcystins plus nodularins, data qualifiers for attribute labeled Total microcystins plus nodularins (ug/L); <; less than symbol indicates concentration was less than the laboratory reporting level.

ADDA specific enzyme-linked immunosorbent assay, recoverable, micrograms per liter (USGS parameter code 89011); refer to prior attribute for associated data qualifier; "NM" indicates value was not measured. Assay name, The assigned name for an assay. An assay is a PCR reaction mix that contains primers to amplify a specific target and a reagent to detect that amplified target.
Dynamic range (copies/rxn), The log concentration range, in copies per reaction volume, of control material over which the standard curve is confirmed to be linear.

Range of amplification efficiencies, (percent), the calculation of efficiency of the PCR amplification. The amplification efficiency is calculated using the slope of the regression line in the standard curve. A slope close to -3.32 indicates optimal, 100% PCR amplification efficiency.

Range of R2 values, Range of regression coefficients calculated from the regression line in the standard curve. The R^2 value indicates the closeness of fit between the standard curve regression line and the individual Ct data points from the standard reactions. A value of 1.00 indicates a perfect fit between the regression line and the data points.

LoD (copies/rxn), ​Limit of detection (LoD) is the lowest concentration that can be detected with 95% confidence. The LoD is determined by running a series of dilutions of the target with a minimum of 10 replicates per dilution.  The dilutions used will vary depending on the sensitivity of the assay. The dilution with the lowest concentration of known target with detections greater than 95 percent is chosen as the LoD. The average Ct value for this dilution is used to calculate a concentration (copies/rxn) using the standard curve run with the dilution series.

LoQ (copies/rxn), Limit of quantification (LoQ) is the lowest concentration that can be accurately quantified. The LoQ is determined by running a series of dilutions of the target with a minimum of 10 replicates per dilution. The dilutions used will vary depending on the sensitivity of the assay. The dilution with the lowest concentration that has a coefficient of variance (CV) below 35% and a Ct standard deviation at or below 0.5 is chosen for the LoQ calculation. The Ct value for the LoQ is calculated as the average Ct value minus two times the associated standard deviation for this dilution and is then used to calculate a concentration (copies/rxn) using the standard curve run with the dilution series.
